prompt:
Use case: style-transfer
Asset type: runtime tactics unit sprite
Primary request: derive a new Tide Legionary unit for the Myrmidons using the line-art concept as structure and the approved Hunter and Spectre as faction style anchors
Subject: a heavy amphibious frontline infantry unit with plated chest armor, broader mass than Hunter and Spectre, a planted combat stance, and a pressure-forged polearm or boarding weapon instead of pistols
Style/medium: moody post-apocalyptic tactics sprite illustration with stronger pixel clustering, chunkier readable shapes, and a painterly pixel-art-inspired finish that survives downscaling cleanly
Composition/framing: single full-body subject, centered, facing slightly left, grounded battlefield-ready stance, transparent background, tight sprite bounds
Lighting/mood: cold bioluminescent gloom with controlled contrast and Myrmidon faction lighting
Color palette: sea green, kelp black, brine cyan, rust leather, dim biotech teal accents
Constraints: keep the aquatic Myrmidon anatomy and crest language, make the silhouette clearly heavier and more melee-first than Hunter, keep it distinct from Spectre by emphasizing armor mass and planted infantry posture, rendered around 109px tall in game, prioritize large readable pixel-like shapes and reduced micro-detail so the sprite holds up at tactical scale, no floor, no scenery, no text
Avoid: human armor proportions, generic sci-fi soldier read, extra characters, extra weapons, glow spam, particles, photorealism, anime gloss, cinematic background, fine noisy texture, oversized empty padding
